name: Aria
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria clung to my arm and cried at the classroom door. The teacher had to peel her off me.
entries.parent_response: I stayed an extra 15 minutes until she calmed down.

entries.situation: Me getting ready to leave for work
entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria followed me from room to room asking when I'd be back and whether I had my phone.
entries.parent_response: I promised to text her at every break.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria locked herself in the bathroom and wouldn't come out until we cancelled our dinner.
Aria called crying at 9pm saying she couldn't sleep without me and begged to come home.
entries.parent_response: My husband drove over to get her.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria asks me to lie with her until she falls asleep, and then I have to leave my bedroom door open.
entries.parent_response: I lie with her every night, sometimes for an hour.

entries.situation: I went to the bathroom at the store
entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria panicked when she couldn't see me and started calling my name loudly.
entries.parent_response: I keep her right next to me at all times now.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria would only go if I stayed the whole time.
entries.parent_response: I stayed and sat in the kitchen.

entries.situation: School field trip with no parents
entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria refused to get on the bus, said her chest hurt and she felt like she'd throw up.
entries.parent_response: I drove her to the museum myself and walked around with the class.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria kept looking over to make sure I was still in the bleachers and came off the field twice to check.
entries.parent_response: I waved and stayed visible the whole practice.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Aria asked if she could just skip it, and when we got there she wouldn't go in without me.
entries.parent_response: I stayed at the party even though it was meant to be drop-off.

situations: Parent leaving or being out of sight
Sleeping alone or away from home
Being left with others (babysitter, playdates, parties)
parental_responses: Cancelling or leaving plans early; retrieving her
Keeping her in sight at all times
safety_behaviors: Requiring the parent present to sleep
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct.
